---
title: "2 - Descriptives"
subtitle: "Summarise the sample and look before you model"
date: today
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

project_root <- getwd()
while (!dir.exists(file.path(project_root, ".git"))) {
  parent <- dirname(project_root)
  if (identical(parent, project_root)) {
    stop("Could not find the project root. Please render from inside the HORIGIN 1B project.")
  }
  project_root <- parent
}

required_packages <- c("haven", "dplyr", "janitor", "ggplot2", "knitr")
to_install <- setdiff(required_packages, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install) > 0) {
  install.packages(to_install, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}
invisible(lapply(required_packages, library, character.only = TRUE))

HO_EXP1b_raw <- haven::read_sav(
  file.path(project_root, "data", "raw", "HO_EXP1b_raw.sav")
)

EXP1b_WORK <- HO_EXP1b_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(Finished == 1) |>
  janitor::clean_names()

EXP1b_WORK$cond <- factor(
  EXP1b_WORK$cond,
  levels = c(0, 1),
  labels = c("no origin", "origin")
)

item_mapping <- list(
  happiness = c("hap1", "hap2", "hap3"),
  desirablity = c("de1", "de2", "de3")
)

scale_names <- paste0(names(item_mapping), "_mean")

EXP1b_WORK[scale_names] <- lapply(
  item_mapping,
  function(items) rowMeans(EXP1b_WORK[, items], na.rm = TRUE)
)
```

This page reads the cleaned dataset produced by
[1 - Data preparation](01-data-preparation.qmd) and describes the sample before
any model is fitted.

## Sample By Condition

```{r}
#| label: tbl-condition-n
#| tbl-cap: "Number of finished participants by experimental condition."
condition_n <- EXP1b_WORK |>
  dplyr::count(cond, name = "n")

knitr::kable(condition_n)
```

## Outcomes By Condition

```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc
#| tbl-cap: "Means and SDs of happiness and desirability, by experimental condition."
descriptives <- EXP1b_WORK |>
  dplyr::group_by(cond) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = dplyr::n(),
    mean_happiness = mean(happiness_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_happiness = stats::sd(happiness_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_desirablity = mean(desirablity_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_desirablity = stats::sd(desirablity_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(descriptives, digits = 2)
```

## Look First

```{r}
#| label: fig-gender-by-cond
#| fig-cap: "Participant gender by experimental condition."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
ggplot2::ggplot(
  EXP1b_WORK,
  ggplot2::aes(x = cond, fill = as.factor(gender))
) +
  ggplot2::geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = "Participants", fill = "Gender") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-happiness-by-cond
#| fig-cap: "Happiness by experimental condition. Red points are condition means; bars are 95% CIs."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
ggplot2::ggplot(
  EXP1b_WORK,
  ggplot2::aes(x = cond, y = happiness_mean)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.25) +
  ggplot2::stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, colour = "firebrick") +
  ggplot2::stat_summary(
    fun.data = ggplot2::mean_cl_normal,
    geom = "errorbar",
    width = 0.15,
    colour = "firebrick"
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = "Happiness mean") +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-happiness-desirability
#| fig-cap: "Desirability versus happiness, coloured by experimental condition."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
ggplot2::ggplot(
  EXP1b_WORK,
  ggplot2::aes(x = desirablity_mean, y = happiness_mean, colour = cond)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_jitter(width = 0.05, height = 0.05, alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Desirability mean",
    y = "Happiness mean",
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

Next: [3 - Analysis](03-analysis.qmd).
